subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic and aim are communicated with clarity and consistency throughout the report
Research question is precisely stated, narrowly focused and directly underpins each section
Methodology is comprehensive, well justified and shows clear evidence of informed selection of standard test methods
weaknesses: Scope of âripstop nylonâ could be narrowed (denier vs weave) to remove minor breadth
Clarification needed on maintaining constant Reynolds range during trials
Environmental variables (temperature, pressure) could be recorded to strengthen methodological rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates exceptionally clear purpose and focus, a sharply defined research question and a fully appropriate, wellâjustified methodology. Minor points of breadth and suggestions for additional controls do not detract from an otherwise flawless alignment of methods to objectives, meriting full marks in Focus and Method.

strengths: Consistently applies advanced drag theory, Reynolds number and Darcyâs law to experimental choices
Equations are correctly derived, symbols defined and units used consistently
Terminology (e.g. volumetric flow rate, drag coefficient, denier) is accurate and sustained throughout
weaknesses: Occasional conflation of form drag and skinâfriction terminology in the background section
Could further clarify flowâseparation concepts when introducing figures to enhance precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student exhibits excellent subject knowledge, integrating multiple theoretical frameworks into a coherent conceptual model and using precise physics terminology throughout. Minor imprecision in one section does not undermine an otherwise exemplary display of understanding, warranting top marks.

strengths: Thorough data processing with uncertainties, error bars and linear regression tightly anchored to the research question
Arguments are logically structured and sophisticated, linking Darcyâs law, turbulence and pressure differentials
Evaluation identifies key systematic and random errors and proposes credible improvements
weaknesses: Analysis rests on only five data points, risking overfitting and limiting depth of interpretation
One sampleâcalculation summation is incomplete (Eqn 15 denominator)
Evaluation remains largely descriptive; a more quantitative uncertainty propagation and deeper methodological critique would strengthen the section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research analysis and argumentation consistently operate at the upper levels of the 7â9 band, with only the evaluation strand showing midâband performance. The overall level of critical thinking is outstanding, though a single area of evaluation could be expanded. This nearâperfect mastery justifies an 11 out of 12.

strengths: Report structure is clear, with a comprehensive table of contents, numbered sections and consistent referencing
Visual elements (schematics, photos, graphs with error bars) are clear, well labelled and effectively integrated
Formatting adheres to EE conventions, including Harvard references and appendices
weaknesses: Some multiâline table cells overflow across pages reducing readability
Figure 3 caption lacks scale or magnification reference
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is highly effective: structured logically, formatted consistently and supported by clear visuals. Only minor layout and caption details prevent a flawless execution. Overall, the work meets the highest standards for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: Physics terminologyâReynolds number, volumetric flow rate, denier, drag coefficient, range uncertaintyâis used accurately, symbols are defined, and equations are manipulated correctly.  Notation is consistent and units accompany values in tables and graphs.  Occasional typographical inconsistencies (âwrapâ vs âwarpâ) are negligible; overall usage is precise and sustained, aligning with the top band, score 5.

The discussion follows a logical structure: background â hypothesis â results â comparison with literature â implication.  Arguments are coherent, evidence-based and largely free of contradictions.  Sophistication is evident in linking Darcyâs law, pressure differentials and turbulence, but nuance regarding alternative explanations or modelling depth is limited; therefore a solid but not exemplary performance within the 7-9 rangeâscore 7.

A dual-stage methodology (ASTM D737 permeability test and sub-sonic wind-tunnel drag test) is described in step-by-step detail, supported by apparatus photographs, justification of controlled variables, sample-size (5 fabrics Ã5 trials) and appropriate data-processing formulae.  Selection of standard test methods, force balance and uncertainty treatment shows informed choice.  Limitations (non-perfect hemisphere, reference-area assumption) are acknowledged but do not indicate major omissions; therefore the work meets the descriptor of a complete, appropriate method, scoring solidly within 5-6âhere awarded 5.

The student integrates drag theory, Reynolds number, Darcyâs law and permeability testing into a coherent conceptual framework, applies them correctly in calculations and links them to experimental choices.  Multiple authoritative sources are cited in-text and in the works-cited list.  Minor slips (e.g., conflating form-drag with skin-friction wording) are present but do not impede understanding, so performance corresponds with âexcellent knowledgeâ though marginally short of exemplary perfectionâscore 5.

The evaluation section identifies key systematic and random errors, explains their impact, and proposes plausible improvements and extensions.  Reflection on reference-area assumptions and shape imperfections shows some critical insight, yet evaluation is mainly descriptive and lacks quantitative error analysis (e.g., propagated uncertainty on Cd) or alternative methodological critiques.  This corresponds to âgood evaluation with some critical analysisââmid-band score 5.

The student states the topic in the title, restates it in the introduction and table of contents, and consistently refers to the same focus throughout.  The aimâto determine how air-permeability affects drag on a hemispherical rip-stop nylon parachuteâis foregrounded, scientifically framed, and never lost during the report.  The writing is concise, logically ordered and supported by clear headings, tables and labelled figures, enabling the reader to follow purpose and scope with ease.  These qualities match the highest descriptor for clarity and effective communication.

Visuals (schematics, apparatus photos, graphs with error bars) are clear, labelled and referenced in the text.  Table layouts generally aid understanding though a few overflow across pages.  Figure numbering is consistent.  Overall the layout supports communication effectively, warranting the upper band; slight presentation lapses justify 3 rather than 4.

The research question is explicitly worded, narrowly focused on one independent variable (air-permeability in cmÂ³ sâ»Â¹ cmâ»Â²) and one dependent variable (coefficient of drag).  Its wording is measurable and directly underpins each later section.  Minor breadth remainsââripstop nylonâ covers five denier grades and might invite questions of thickness versus weaveâbut the overall focus and alignment to the discussion are strong, meriting the upper band though not flawlessly âtight,â hence 5 rather than 6.

Data are processed correctly (conversion factors, means, uncertainties), plotted with error bars and linear regression, and quantitatively interpreted (gradient, RÂ², propagation of slope uncertainty).  Analysis remains clearly anchored to the research question and draws on theory to explain the negative correlation.  However, depth is constrained by only five data points and a limited exploration of confounding factors, so the work sits at the lower edge of the 7-9 bandâscore 7.

The essay is fully structured with title page, contents, numbered sections, tables, figures, appendices and consistent Harvard-style references.  Formatting adheres to EE conventions and facilitates navigation.  Minor spacing/line-break glitches in copied tables do not detract from clarity; hence top band, score 4.

annotations.comment: Introduction clearly explains personal motivation and frames the aim, aiding reader engagement and context.
The research question is clearly stated, measurable and directly underpins each section, demonstrating focused inquiry.
Word count is under the 4000-word limit and is correctly placed on the title page, meeting formatting requirements.
The table of contents is comprehensive and wellâorganized, facilitating navigation through the report.
Multiâline table cells overflow across pages and reduce readability; consider simplifying or splitting long justifications.
The specified Reynolds range is broad; clarify how these extremes are maintained constant during trials.
The hypothesis succinctly links Darcyâs law to an expected negative correlation, showing strong critical thinking.
Consider quantifying the expected slope from theoretical models to strengthen the hypothesis.
Controlled-variables table provides comprehensive justifications but overflows pages and diminishes clarity.
Consider briefly previewing key variables or methodology in the introduction to orient the reader to the experimental design.
The background section correctly differentiates drag components but later conflates form drag and skin friction; revise terminology for precision.
When introducing Figure 1, briefly define flow separation to strengthen conceptual clarity.
Rearranged drag equation (Eqn 2) is correctly derived and displayed with consistent notation.
Background on Darcyâs law and permeability (Eqn 4) is well integrated into theoretical framework.
Include explicit unit consistency for area and thickness in Eqn 4 to avoid unit conversion ambiguity.
Apparatus description for ASTM D737 test is detailed and justified, showing informed methodological choice.
Figure 3 caption lacks scale or magnification; include reference dimensions for clarity.
Prioritize the most critical controlled variables to focus analysis and simplify presentation.
Wind-tunnel procedure is systematic and detailed, reflecting strong methodological planning.
Include recording of environmental variables (temperature, pressure) in the wind-tunnel setup to account for density variations.
Raw data tables include instrument uncertainty and replicate trials, demonstrating thorough data collection.
Sample calculations are clearly presented, but Eqn 15âs summation appears to omit one term; correct the denominator.
Processed results table succinctly communicates final values with uncertainties, enhancing clarity of outcomes.
Graph 1 analysis cites RÂ² but should discuss potential overfitting given only five data points.
Evaluation of systematic errors is insightful, but the reference-area improvement lacks completion; detail the high-speed camera method.
Works-cited list is in Harvard style but URLs are broken over lines; ensure each reference displays full URL on one line.
Duplicate entries for the same article appear in the reference list; remove redundancies to improve accuracy.
